content.problemStatement:
Welcome to my nightmare, I think you're going to like it.

After Halloween, Chef had a nightmare. He dreamt that his Halloween candies were secretly operated on, transforming them into tiny monsters that chased him through the night.

To wake up from this nightmare, he needs to solve an interesting problem consisting of a ghastly operation.

There are two arrays AAA and BBB, both of length NNN.

Chef can perform the following operation on these arrays:


Select an index iii ( 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N)
Perform the ghastly operation on index iii, that modifies the arrays as follows:

Let the arrays be
A=[A1,A2,A3,…,AN]B=[B1,B2,B3,…,BN]A = [A_1, A_2, A_3, \ldots, A_N] \\
B = [B_1, B_2, B_3, \ldots, B_N]A=[A1​,A2​,A3​,…,AN​]B=[B1​,B2​,B3​,…,BN​]After operating on index iii, they turn into

A=[A1,A2,…,Ai−1,Ai+1,Ai+2,…,AN,BN]B=[B1,B2,…,Bi−1,Ai,Bi,Bi+1,Bi+2,…,BN−1]A = [A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_{i-1}, A_{i+1}, A_{i+2}, \ldots, A_N, B_N] \\
B = [B_1, B_2, \ldots, B_{i-1}, A_i, B_i, B_{i+1}, B_{i+2}, \ldots, B_{N-1}]A=[A1​,A2​,…,Ai−1​,Ai+1​,Ai+2​,…,AN​,BN​]B=[B1​,B2​,…,Bi−1​,Ai​,Bi​,Bi+1​,Bi+2​,…,BN−1​]Formally, the following steps are performed:


Delete BNB_NBN​ from BBB, and append it to AAA.
Delete AiA_iAi​ from AAA, and insert it before BiB_iBi​ in BBB.
Chef can perform at most 250 000250\, 000250000 such operations.

Is it possible to turn array AAA into a palindrome?

If it is, also find a sequence of operations to do so.

It can be proved that if there is a way to make AAA a palindrome, it can be done so using at most 250 000250\, 000250000 operations.

If there are multiple possible palindromes that AAA can be made into, you may reach any of them.

Note that you **do not need to minimize** the number of operations used.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer NNN — the length of both the arrays.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​: the elements of array AAA.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,…,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1​,B2​,…,BN​: the elements of array BBB.


Output Format
If it is impossible to convert AAA into a palindrome within 250 000250\, 000250000 moves, print −1-1−1.


Otherwise, output two lines.



The first line should contain an integer KKK (0≤K≤250 0000 \leq K \leq 250\, 0000≤K≤250000) — the number of moves you're going to perform.
The second line should contain KKK space-separated integers. The iii-th of them should denote the index chosen in the iii-th operation.

If there are multiple solutions, you may output any of them.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Initially, the arrays are
A=[1,2,3,4,5]A = [1, 2, 3, 4, 5]A=[1,2,3,4,5]
B=[5,4,3,1,2]B = [5, 4, 3, 1, 2]B=[5,4,3,1,2].
Operate on i=4i = 4i=4, which turns the arrays into
A=[1,2,3,5,2]A = [1, 2, 3, 5, 2]A=[1,2,3,5,2]
B=[5,4,3,4,1]B = [5, 4, 3, 4, 1]B=[5,4,3,4,1]
Operate on i=4i = 4i=4 again to obtain
A=[1,2,3,2,1]A = [1, 2, 3, 2, 1]A=[1,2,3,2,1]
B=[5,4,3,5,4]B = [5, 4, 3, 5, 4]B=[5,4,3,5,4]
AAA is now a palindrome, so we're done.
Note that BBB isn't a palindrome, which is OK.

Explanation:
There no way to convert AAA into a palindrome.
